sourcePriority: Official Rules prevail over the marketing page, challenge overview, and optional Devpost plugin.
selectedTrackRationale: PolicyTwin is a testing, agentic workflow, and policy-to-code verification tool for developers.

eligibility.location: Republic of Korea appears in the eligible territory list and eligibility also requires current OpenAI API support.
eligibility.age: Entrant must be at least the age of majority where they reside; a parent or guardian may enter for an under-age student.
eligibility.teams: Eligible individuals, teams, and qualifying organizations may enter; a team or organization must appoint an authorized representative.

projectRequirements: Build a working project with Codex and GPT-5.6 in one eligible track.
The project must install and run consistently and function as depicted in the submission.
New projects are allowed; pre-existing projects must be meaningfully extended after the submission period starts and clearly distinguish prior and new work with evidence.
Third-party SDKs, APIs, data, and open-source components require authorization and license compliance.
submissionRequirements: Provide an English project description explaining features and functionality.
Provide a public YouTube demonstration video shorter than three minutes with clear audio showing the project and use of Codex and GPT-5.6.
Provide a repository URL that is public with relevant licensing or private and shared with testing@devpost.com and build-week-event@openai.com.
README must explain Codex collaboration, acceleration, key decisions, and how GPT-5.6 and Codex contributed.
Provide the /feedback Codex Session ID for the project thread where most core functionality was built.
Developer tools must include installation instructions, supported platforms, and a judge-ready demo, sandbox, test account, or equivalent path that does not require rebuilding from scratch.
Provide free, unrestricted working-project access for judging through the end of the judging period.
Submission materials must be in English or include English translations.

plugin.rule: The Devpost Hackathons plugin is optional. Official Rules and the Hackathon Website prevail over plugin output.

knownDiscrepancies: The OpenAI marketing page shows judging through August 7, while the Official Rules end judging on August 5 at 17:00 PT; the Official Rules control.
